commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No automated code repair remains; the blocker is maintainer/reporter live-provider proof or an explicit proof waiver before merge.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the diff changes a local resolver option and a focused Vitest assertion without new dependencies, secrets handling, or code execution paths. (sha=506f4bfc87e00368f6c3a5abcdb13a44cc905352)
commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens embedded-agent skip-discovery model resolution to allow bundled static catalog fallback for OpenClaw harness runs and adds a Google Vertex regression assertion.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +27. Total +27 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main gates bundled static fallback off for default OpenClaw harness skip-discovery resolution, while the resolver only consults the static catalog when that option is true. I did not run a live Google Vertex API-key repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair remains; the blocker is maintainer/reporter live-provider proof or an explicit proof waiver before merge.

target.title: fix(agent): use static catalog for skip-agent model resolution
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes the skip-agent-discovery model-resolution path so known provider-owned models can use the bundled static catalog before OpenClaw models.json is generated.
- This prevents known Google Vertex model refs such as google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro from surfacing a misleading missing models.providers registration error when the static catalog already knows the model.
- Adds regression coverage for the Google Vertex run path.

## Linked context
Closes #88816

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro could fail before reply with an Unknown model / missing provider registration error while skip-agent-discovery resolution was active.
- Real environment tested: Linux contributor checkout of OpenClaw, focused runner test harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
- Evidence after fix: the focused Vitest shard passed with 112 tests, including the new regression asserting the google-vertex run path passes allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback with skipAgentDiscovery.
- Observed result after fix: skip-agent-discovery resolution is permitted to consult the bundled static catalog for known model refs.
- What was not tested: a live Google Vertex API-key request against the external service.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I do not have a safe Google Vertex API key/project in this contributor environment, so the live provider call is left for maintainer or reporter confirmation.
- Before evidence: issue #88816 includes the reported failing error for google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro and google-vertex/gemini-3.5-flash.

## Tests and validation
- PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
- corepack pnpm oxfmt src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts (via commit hook)
- git diff --check

## Risk checklist
- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, known static-catalog models can resolve earlier in the skip-agent-discovery path instead of waiting for OpenClaw model discovery or erroring.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No secrets or network behavior changes; this only allows local bundled model metadata fallback during model resolution.
- Highest-risk area: resolving a static-catalog model slightly earlier than before. Mitigation: the fallback only applies to known bundled catalog entries, and existing full model discovery remains available for runtime-generated models.

## Current review state
- Next action: maintainer review and, ideally, reporter/maintainer confirmation with a real Google Vertex API-key profile.
- Waiting on: CI and any maintainer-requested real-provider proof.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
target.author: charles-openclaw

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
